text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH CONTRAST
RESTAGING / PRE-SYSTEMIC THERAPY BASELINE EXAMINATION

CLINICAL INDICATION:
63-year-old female with known stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC), right kidney primary. Patient underwent cytoreductive right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection approximately 6 weeks prior. Known bilateral pulmonary metastases on pre-operative staging imaging. This examination serves as restaging and baseline assessment prior to initiation of first-line systemic immunotherapy (ipilimumab plus nivolumab). Evaluate for post-surgical changes, local recurrence, residual/interval change in pulmonary metastatic disease, and any new sites of metastatic involvement.

COMPARISON:
Prior CT chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast (initial staging examination, performed at time of diagnosis, approximately 8 weeks prior to this study).

TECHNIQUE:
Multi-detector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed in standard fashion using intravenous iodinated contrast material (Isovue-370, 110 mL, IV, antecubital access). Acquisitions obtained in the portal venous phase for abdomen and pelvis; dedicated arterial phase chest acquisition. Multiplanar reformats in coronal and sagittal planes reviewed in addition to axial source images. Scout radiograph reviewed. Oral contrast was not administered. Adequate renal function confirmed prior to contrast administration (creatinine 1.3 mg/dL, eGFR 46 mL/min/1.73m2; note single kidney status post right nephrectomy).

--- FINDINGS ---

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways:
The lungs are well-expanded bilaterally. No pneumothorax. No pleural effusion. No pleural thickening or nodularity.

Pulmonary Nodules / Metastatic Disease:
Multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules are identified, consistent with known metastatic disease. Designated target lesions per RECIST 1.1 criteria are as follows:

  Target Lesion 1: Right lower lobe, posterior basal segment — solid nodule measuring 1.8 cm in greatest axial dimension (1.8 x 1.5 cm). Well-circumscribed, subpleural. Morphologically unchanged in size and appearance compared to prior staging examination. No internal cavitation or calcification.

  Target Lesion 2: Right lower lobe, lateral basal segment — solid nodule measuring 0.9 cm in greatest axial dimension. Well-circumscribed. Stable compared to prior.

  Target Lesion 3: Left lower lobe, posterobasal segment — solid nodule measuring 0.7 cm. Well-circumscribed, peripheral. Stable.

  Non-target Lesion A: Right lower lobe, medial basal segment — subcentimeter solid nodule measuring 0.4 cm. Stable.

  Non-target Lesion B: Left lower lobe, lateral basal segment — subcentimeter nodule measuring 0.4 cm. Stable.

No new pulmonary nodules identified. No coalescence of nodules. No airspace consolidation. No endobronchial lesions. No lymphangitic spread pattern.

Sum of target lesion diameters (RECIST 1.1): 3.4 cm.

This sum is designated as the baseline measurement for response assessment with planned systemic immunotherapy.

Mediastinum and Hila:
The mediastinum is of normal width. No mediastinal lymphadenopathy (no lymph nodes measuring >1.0 cm in short axis). No hilar adenopathy. No pericardial effusion. Cardiac silhouette is normal in size and configuration. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber. No evidence of pulmonary arterial dilatation.

Bones (Chest):
No lytic or blastic osseous lesions identified in the visualized thoracic skeleton. No pathological fractures. Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine without suspicious vertebral lesions.

---

ABDOMEN:

Post-Surgical Changes:
Status post right radical nephrectomy with en bloc right adrenal gland resection and retroperitoneal lymph node dissection. A surgical clip cluster is identified at the right renal hilum and right retroperitoneal operative bed. Post-surgical fluid collection is not identified; no organized hematoma or abscess in the right retroperitoneum. Fascial thickening and mild soft tissue stranding in the right retroperitoneal fat are present, consistent with expected post-operative changes. No evidence of anastomotic leak or bowel-related complication.

Right Nephrectomy Bed:
No discrete soft tissue mass or nodular enhancement at the right nephrectomy bed to suggest local recurrence. The residual retroperitoneal fat demonstrates expected post-operative architectural distortion. The right adrenal fossa is empty, consistent with prior adrenalectomy performed en bloc.

Left Kidney:
Solitary left kidney with compensatory hypertrophy (longitudinal diameter 12.4 cm). Normal parenchymal enhancement, cortical thickness maintained. No hydronephrosis. No cortical lesion or mass. No perinephric fluid. No urothelial thickening.

Retroperitoneal Lymph Nodes:
No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy identified. The previously noted right hilar/paracaval retroperitoneal lymph node (2.4 cm on staging CT) has been surgically resected. No new lymph nodes measuring >1.0 cm in short axis in the para-aortic, paracaval, aortocaval, or common iliac chains.

Liver:
The liver is normal in size with homogeneous parenchymal enhancement. No focal hepatic lesions. No intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. Portal and hepatic veins are patent.

Gallbladder and Biliary System:
Gallbladder is present, normal in appearance, without wall thickening, pericholecystic fluid, or cholelithiasis. Common bile duct is not dilated.

Pancreas:
The pancreas demonstrates normal morphology and enhancement throughout the head, body, and tail. No pancreatic ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic fluid or mass.

Spleen:
The spleen is normal in size (long axis 11.3 cm) and homogeneous in attenuation. No focal splenic lesions. No splenomegaly.

Left Adrenal Gland:
The left adrenal gland is normal in size and configuration. No adrenal nodule or mass. No adrenal metastasis.

Aorta and Major Vessels:
The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber throughout. Celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and bilateral renal arteries (left) are patent. The left renal vein is patent without thrombosis. No inferior vena cava abnormality; the right renal vein resection margin is surgical in appearance without evidence of residual or recurrent IVC thrombus. No new venous thrombosis.

Gastrointestinal Tract:
The stomach, small bowel, and visualized colon are normal in caliber and wall thickness. No bowel obstruction. No evidence of bowel wall thickening or mucosal irregularity. No mesenteric or omental nodularity.

Peritoneum / Ascites:
No peritoneal implants. No ascites.

---

PELVIS:

Bladder:
The urinary bladder is partially distended with normal wall thickness and smooth contour. No bladder mass or filling defect. No urothelial lesion.

Gynecologic Structures:
The uterus appears normal in size for age. Both ovaries are not definitively visualized (within normal limits for postmenopausal patient). No pelvic mass.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes:
No pelvic lymphadenopathy.

Bones (Pelvis and Lumbar Spine):
No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions in the pelvis, sacrum, or visualized lumbar spine. No pathological fractures. Mild degenerative changes involving the lumbar facet joints and sacroiliac joints are noted.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. STATUS POST RIGHT RADICAL NEPHRECTOMY WITH RETROPERITONEAL LYMPH NODE DISSECTION: Expected post-operative changes at the right nephrectomy bed without evidence of local recurrence. No organized post-operative fluid collection or hematoma. Solitary left kidney with compensatory hypertrophy.

2. RESIDUAL BILATERAL PULMONARY METASTATIC DISEASE: Three designated target lesions per RECIST 1.1 are present and stable compared to pre-operative staging CT: right lower lobe dominant nodule (1.8 cm, TL1), right lower lobe second nodule (0.9 cm, TL2), and left lower lobe nodule (0.7 cm, TL3). Two additional subcentimeter non-target pulmonary nodules identified bilaterally, all stable. Sum of target lesion diameters 3.4 cm — designated as RECIST 1.1 baseline for planned first-line systemic therapy.

3. NO RETROPERITONEAL LYMPHADENOPATHY: The previously sampled right retroperitoneal hilar lymph node (2.4 cm) has been resected; no new retroperitoneal or pelvic lymphadenopathy.

4. NO NEW SITES OF METASTATIC DISEASE: No hepatic, adrenal, osseous, or peritoneal metastases identified. No new intrathoracic lymphadenopathy.

5. SOLITARY LEFT KIDNEY: Intact with compensatory hypertrophy, no evidence of intrinsic renal pathology.

Overall assessment: Metastatic disease limited to bilateral pulmonary nodules following cytoreductive nephrectomy. This examination is suitable as a RECIST 1.1 baseline prior to initiation of systemic immunotherapy.
